text: ABN AMRO Editorial. The European NIS2 guideline mentions it nine times: cyber hygiene. That's telling. It is not an option, but an absolute condition for working digitally safely. Entrepreneurs who take cyber hygiene seriously reduce the risk of cyber attacks and lay a strong foundation for sustainable digital resilience.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text: The European NIS2 guideline mentions it nine times: cyber hygiene. That's telling. It is not an option, but an absolute condition for working digitally safely. Entrepreneurs who take cyber hygiene seriously reduce the risk of cyber attacks and lay a strong foundation for sustainable digital resilience. Cyber ​​hygiene revolves around the basic measures that every organization must have in place: strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, regular updates, reliable backups and employee alertness.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

But it is also about daily habits.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text: It is not an option, but an absolute condition for working digitally safely. Entrepreneurs who take cyber hygiene seriously reduce the risk of cyber attacks and lay a strong foundation for sustainable digital resilience. Cyber ​​hygiene revolves around the basic measures that every organization must have in place: strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, regular updates, reliable backups and employee alertness. But it is also about daily habits. Consider immediately reporting suspicious emails, locking your screen when you walk away or avoiding private devices without security.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text: Entrepreneurs who take cyber hygiene seriously reduce the risk of cyber attacks and lay a strong foundation for sustainable digital resilience. Cyber ​​hygiene revolves around the basic measures that every organization must have in place: strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, regular updates, reliable backups and employee alertness. But it is also about daily habits. Consider immediately reporting suspicious emails, locking your screen when you walk away or avoiding private devices without security. The basics are often forgotten.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text: Cyber ​​hygiene revolves around the basic measures that every organization must have in place: strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, regular updates, reliable backups and employee alertness. But it is also about daily habits. Consider immediately reporting suspicious emails, locking your screen when you walk away or avoiding private devices without security. metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text: But it is also about daily habits. Consider immediately reporting suspicious emails, locking your screen when you walk away or avoiding private devices without security. metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text:
Old accounts of former employees sometimes remain active. People often think: “We are not interesting for hackers.”
Take cyber hygiene seriously. Cyber criminals focus on companies that do not have the basics in order. A forgotten update or a weak password is often enough to get in. And once inside, one infected laptop can shut down an entire organization.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text:
People often think: “We are not interesting for hackers.”
Take cyber hygiene seriously. Cyber criminals focus on companies that do not have the basics in order. A forgotten update or a weak password is often enough to get in. And once inside, one infected laptop can shut down an entire organization. With the NIS2 Quality Mark (NIS2 QM) you show that your organization has properly arranged the basics.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​hygiene: the basis of digital security

text: Cyber criminals focus on companies that do not have the basics in order. A forgotten update or a weak password is often enough to get in. And once inside, one infected laptop can shut down an entire organization. With the NIS2 Quality Mark (NIS2 QM) you show that your organization has properly arranged the basics. The quality mark confirms that you meet the requirements of good cyber hygiene, checked by an independent audit.
